channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Sabedra: California high school basketball season ends with performance for ages
item.description: Riordan coach Joey Curtin on Roosevelt's Brayden Burries: 'He was getting to the rim in transition. Getting to his mid-range. Walking into 3s. That's how you get 44 points on 22 shots.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sabedra, The Mercury News

item.title: Expanding their reach: Even with extra travel here’s why Cal, Stanford say first basketball season in ACC was a success
item.description: Next season most of the ACC’s biggest men's basketball brands – Duke, North Carolina, Louisville and Clemson -- will play at Stanford and Cal.
item.dc:creator: Mike Barber, The Mercury News

item.title: Dodgers' Mookie Betts still struggling with illness, status for opening day in doubt
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers shortstop Mookie Betts is still struggling to recover from an illness, with manager Dave Roberts pessimistic that the eight-time All-Star will be ready for opening day against the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday in Japan.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McLaren's Lando Norris wins wet and wild Australian Grand Prix. Hamilton finishes 10th
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — McLaren’s Lando Norris won a chaotic rain-affected Australian Grand Prix, his first victory at Albert Park, with the British driver just managing to stay ahead of Red Bull’s Max Verstappen following a third safety car late in Sunday's season-opening Formula 1 race.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Frustrating start for Lewis Hamilton and Ferrari at F1 Australian Grand Prix
item.description: Lewis Hamilton’s relationship with his new Ferrari team was pressure tested in Australia, where he finished a disappointing 10th on debut, and was frustrated by a bad strategy that cost him a potential victory, and constant radio chatter throughout the difficult race won by McLaren’s Lando Norris.
item.dc:creator: Stewart Bell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Real Salt Lake earns 2-1 victory at Houston
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake scored two goals in the first half and successfully held on to defeat the Houston Dynamo 2-1 for a road victory Saturday night. Diogo Goncalves and Diego Luna each punched in goals to lead the offensive attack for RSL (2-2-0, six points).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bickerstaff blasts officials after Pistons receive 5 technical fouls in loss to Thunder
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff blasted the officials after Detroit was called for five technical fouls in the third quarter of a 113-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about March Madness if you don’t follow college basketball
item.description: The college basketball season is winding down, which means March Madness is upon us. The annual men's and women's National Collegiate Athletic Association tournaments are among the most closely watched sporting events in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIDGET BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 6 St. John's beats Creighton 82-66 for 1st Big East Tournament crown in 25 years
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Regular-season champs. A tournament title. Pride in the program fully restored. The pied piper of college basketball rebuilds, Rick Pitino has St. John's back atop the Big East. And this time, the nets were coming down. RJ Luis Jr.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suns' Bradley Beal still 'smiling every day' amid tumultuous season, but open to offseason trade
item.description: The Phoenix Suns were actively seeking to include Bradley Beal in a trade package last month, but the All-Star guard appears open to moving in the offseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tonje scores 32 as No. 18 Wisconsin tops No. 7 Michigan St. 77-74 in Big Ten semis
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — John Tonje scored 32 points and grabbed seven rebounds to help No. 18 Wisconsin beat No. 7 Michigan State 77-74 on Saturday in the semifinals of the Big Ten Tournament. John Blackwell added 14 points as the Badgers (26-8) won for the third time in four days. They will play No.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick's 24-year-old girlfriend leaks message criticizing her relationship
item.description: Jordon Hudson, Bill Belichick's 24-year-old girlfriend, posted a screenshot of a hostile exchange between her and someone who criticized the couple's relationship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Falcons sticking with Kirk Cousins for now, plan to pay roster bonus: report
item.description: The Atlanta Falcons' front office has decided to keep veteran quarterback Kirk Cousins on the roster through Saturday, which triggers a guaranteed $10 million bonus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UNLV fires coach Kevin Kruger, one of the program's links to its glory-day past
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Coach Kevin Kruger, who led UNLV to the Sweet 16 as the team's point guard in 2007 while playing for his father, Hall of Famer Lon Kruger, was fired by the Rebels on Saturday. Kruger went 76-55 in four seasons and failed to lead the Rebels to the NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clemson gets locked out of locker room in ACC semifinal; lose narrowly to Louisville
item.description: The Clemson Tigers were forced to gear up for the last 20 minutes of their ACC semifinal game from the halls of Spectrum Center after their locker room was locked.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lewis Hamilton 8th in qualifying in Ferrari debut as Norris takes pole for F1 race in Australia
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — McLaren locked up the front row in Melbourne for Sunday’s Australian Grand Prix, having dominated its rivals, including Red Bull, Mercedes, and Ferrari — whose drivers, Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton, will start from seventh and eighth on the grid.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Smoking areas in clubhouse, no pitch clock: MLB players get a little taste of baseball in Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The Chicago Cubs and Los Angeles Dodgers can party like it's 2022 on the baseball field for a few days in Japan, and even like it's 1982 in the clubhouse. There are a lot of similarities between baseball in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Withers' lane violation adds improbable twist to UNC's failed comeback against No. 1 Duke in ACCs
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jae'Lyn Withers stood in a hallway of the Spectrum Center, his left arm resting around the shoulders of North Carolina coach Hubert Davis. He was surrounded by reporters, all wanting to know a simple question.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WNBA great Candace Parker highlights late Tennessee coach Pat Summitt's 'truly important' legacy
item.description: Former Tennessee star Candace Parker reflected on Pat Summitt's legacy and shared how she continues to apply the life lessons the legendary coach taught her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Women's soccer team to change name after transgender backlash to marketing campaign
item.description: NWSL expansion team BOS Nation FC will be changing its name after backlash to a marketing campaign it tried to launch back in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poway and USD alum Connor Joe to play for his hometown Padres
item.description: As the San Diego Padres are gearing up for the start of the 2025 MLB season, a player who has his sights set on making the Opening Day roster with his hometown Friars is Poway resident Connor Joe.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Third MLB hat pulled over accidental vulgarity as others sell for hundreds online
item.description: Yet another newly released MLB hat has been pulled from online stores after fans noted the potentially crass design the cap sported.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Rays 'cannot move forward' with new ballpark deal, owner says
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays will not be getting a new stadium in St. Petersburg under the existing deal, the team announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah moves closer to playoff berth with 3-2 victory
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - After blowing leads in their two previous meetings with the Anaheim Ducks this season, the Utah Hockey Club wasn't about to let it happen a third time.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The 49ers agree to a 2-year contract with QB Mac Jones, AP source says
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Quarterback Mac Jones will finally join the San Francisco 49ers four years after the team nearly drafted him third overall.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Johnson: Jackson and Thuney are 'tough,' 'gritty' and 'dirty' players the Bears need on the O-line
item.description: The Chicago Bears have signed Jonah Jackson and Joe Thuney, two players with connections to General Manager Ryan Poles and offensive line coach Ben Johnson, to enhance the team's offensive line in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, NewsNation

item.title: Jazz losing skid hits six after 114-108 loss at Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) - Sam Hauser made nine 3-pointers — seven in the third quarter — and finished with a career-high 33 points to lead the short-handed Boston Celtics past the Utah Jazz 114-108 on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Bears sign DE Dayo Odeyingbo to 3-year deal
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Bears have signed defensive end Dayo Odeyingbo to a three-year deal, according to NFL insider Ian Rapoport. The 25-year-old was a second-round pick by the Colts in 2021 and addresses a need the team had on the defensive line.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Rugby: Cathedral Catholic 28, La Costa Canyon 17
item.description:
The High School Boys Rugby Championship was held in Del Mar as the Cathedral Catholic Dons hosted the La Costa Canyon Mavericks.
It was the Dons who came out victorious, defeated La Costa Canyon by a score of 28-17, to win their 5th straight Rugby Championship.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Soldier Field to host Premier League doubleheader this summer
item.description: The Premier League Summer Series is coming to Chicago this summer, featuring four Premier League clubs - AFC Bournemouth, Everton, Manchester United, and West Ham United - in a doubleheader at Soldier Field on Wednesday, July 30.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
